title: vigna unguiculata subsp. sesquipedalis
intro: Vigna unguiculata subsp. sesquipedalis, also known as the yardlong bean or Chinese long bean, is a variety of the cowpea plant native to Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiviral effects, as well as its ability to lower blood sugar levels and reduce blood pressure. The yardlong bean has been associated with various health benefits, including improved cardiovascular health, reduced risk of chronic diseases, and enhanced immune function. In herbal preparations, the yardlong bean can be used in teas, infusions, and decoctions to take advantage of its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
